SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER; LAURA REDING, CSR	711

OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY ORDER OR ALTERNATIVE MANDATE SETTING ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT DECISION AND ORDER BY THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA REVOKING PETITIONERS MEDICAL LICENSE WITH POS (TRANSACTION ID # 17206038) FILED BY PETITIONER ADAMS, ALFRED EUGENE

LAW AND MOTION 302,RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY OR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY PETITIONER ALFRED ADAMS IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER IS BASED ON THE CONTENTIONS THAT DR. ADAMS WAS PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE DEFAULT DECISION AND ORDER AND DR. ADAMS' PETITION WAS UNTIMELY FILED. WHEN THE AMENDED PETITION IS READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE DOCUMENTS THAT THE BOARD HAS REQUESTED THAT THE COURT TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE, DR. ADAMS HAS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT DEFAULT DECISION AND ORDER WERE NOT PROPERLY SERVED PER GOVERNMENT CODE 8311. SECTION 8311 WAS RECENTLY AMENDED TO REQUIRE A RECEIPT BE PROVIDED IN ORDER TO CONSTITUTE PROPER SERVICE PER THAT SECTION. THE JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE DOCUMENTS SHOW THAT DR. ADAMS DID NOT SIGN A RECEIPT FOR THE SERVICE OF THE DEFAULT DECISION AND ORDER. THE FACE OF THE AMENDED PETITION DOES NOT NECESSARILY SHOW THAT DR. ADAMS' FILING OF HIS PETITION COMMENCING THIS CASE ON APRIL 7, 2017 WAS UNTIMELY. DR. ADAMS' AMENDED PETITION ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT THIS CASE WAS TIMELY FILED. HANSEN V. BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING (2012) 208 CAL.APP.4TH 664, 669 HELD THAT, PER GOVERNMENT CODE 11523, THE DEADLINE TO SEEK JUDICIAL REVIEW BY PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS THE AGGREGATE OF THE 30-DAY TIME PERIOD IN GOVERNMENT CODE 11521 PLUS THE ADDITIONAL 30-DAY TIME PERIOD IN SECTION 11523. BASED ON DR. ADAMS' ALLEGATION THAT HE DID NOT DISCOVER THAT HIS LICENSE WAS REVOKED UNTIL MARCH 3, 2017, HE HAD UNTIL APRIL 3, 2017 TO SEEK RECONSIDERATION BY THE BOARD AND UNTIL MAY 3, 2017 TO FILE HIS PETITION IN THIS COURT. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GORDON F. AIAVAO, CSR NO. 11216, EMAIL: GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER ALFRED ADAMS' MOTION ON AMENDED PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY OR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE SETTING ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT DECISION AND ORDER BY THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA REVOKING PETITIONERS MEDICAL LICENSE IS GRANTED. DR. ADAMS HAS SHOWN THAT HE WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED WITH ANY OF THE DOCUMENTS LEADING UP TO AND INCLUDING THE DEFAULT DECISION AND ORDER AND HIS PETITION WAS TIMELY FILED PER GOVERNMENT CODE 11523. BASED ON THIS SHOWING, HE IS ENTITLED TO A WRIT SETTING ASIDE THE DEFAULT DECISION AND ORDER BY THE MEDICAL BOARD. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GORDON F. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion On Amended Petition For Peremptory Or Alternative Writ Of Mandate Setting Aside The Default And Default Decision And Order By The Medical Board Of California Revoking Petitioners Medical License
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Robert W. Stewart of the law offices of Robert Wesley Stewart (415-479-3600) appears as counsel for petitioner/moving party

Alfred Eugene Adams, petitioner is present with counsel

Deputy Attorney General, Greg W. Chambers of the California Department of Justice (415-703-5723) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party, Medical Board of California, Department of Consumer Affairs, State of California

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

PETITIONER ALFRED ADAMS' Motion On Amended Petition For Peremptory Or Alternative Writ Of Mandate Setting Aside The Default And Default Decision And Order By The Medical Board Of California Revoking Petitioners Medical License is GRANTED. Dr. Adams has shown that he was not properly served with any of the documents leading up to and including the default decision and order and his petition was timely filed per Government Code 11523. Based on this showing, he is entitled to a writ setting aside the default decision and order by the Medical Board. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Robert W. Stewart of the law offices of Robert Wesley Stewart (415-479-3600) appears as counsel for petitioner/moving party

Alfred Eugene Adams, petitioner is present with counsel

Deputy Attorney General, Greg W. Chambers of the California Department of Justice (415-703-5723) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party, Medical Board of California, Department of Consumer Affairs, State of California

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER to the amended petition for peremptory or alternative writ of mandate filed by petitioner Alfred Adams is OVERRULED. The demurrer is based on the contentions that Dr. Adams was properly served with the default decision and order and Dr. Adams' petition was untimely filed. When the amended petition is read in conjunction with the documents that the Board has requested that the court take judicial notice, Dr. Adams has sufficiently alleged that default decision and order were not properly served per Government Code 8311. Section 8311 was recently amended to require a receipt be provided in order to constitute proper service per that section. The judicially noticeable documents show that Dr. Adams did not sign a receipt for the service of the default decision and order. The face of the amended petition does not necessarily show that Dr. Adams' filing of his petition commencing this case on April 7, 2017 was untimely. Dr. Adams' amended petition adequately alleges that this case was timely filed. Hansen v. Board of Registered Nursing (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 664, 669 held that, per Government Code 11523, the deadline to seek judicial review by petition for writ of mandate is the aggregate of the 30-day time period in Government Code 11521 plus the additional 30-day time period in section 11523. Based on Dr. Adams' allegation that he did not discover that his license was revoked until March 3, 2017, he had until April 3, 2017 to seek reconsideration by the Board and until May 3, 2017 to file his petition in this court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Re Hearing Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER ALFRED ADAMS' Motion Re Hearing Of Writ Of Mandate is Off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter; Laura Reding, CSR 711 =(302/hk)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER to the petition for peremptory or alternative writ of mandate filed by petitioner Alfred Adams is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend. Dr. Adams' argument that Government Code 8311 requires personal service lacks merit. That section permits service by certified mail. On its face Dr. Adams' petition is time-barred due to his failure to file the petition by March 20, 2017 as required by Government Code 11523. However, it appears that Dr. Adams may not have known about the Board's default decision and order until some time after that decision was served on him and thus he may be able to avail himself of the discovery rule. Hansen v. Board of Registered Nursing (2012) 208 Cal. App. 4th 664, 670-71 and 673 leaves open the question of whether the discovery rule applies to section 11523, although that case acknowledges that revocation of a professional license due to adherence to strict time deadlines is a hardship. (Id. at 675). Given this hardship and California case law stating that the "discovery rule has been applied in 'cases where it is manifestly unjust to deprive plaintiffs of a cause of action before they are aware they have been injured,'" (Brisbane Lodging, L.P. v. Webcor Builders, Inc. (2013) 216 Cal. App. 4th 1249, 1257, quoting Leaf v. City of San Mateo (1980) 104 Cal. App. 3d 398, 407), in the absence of any on-point authority to the contrary, this court holds that the discovery rule is applicable to Government Code 11523. Consequently, Dr. Adams is given leave, if he can do so in good faith, to file a first amended petition alleging ultimate facts that he discovered his claims within the limitations period of section 11523. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter; Laura Reding, CSR 711 =(302/hk)
